title: NOC 21202 – Urban and Land Use Planners | GTR Immigration
description: Urban and Land Use Planners shape sustainable communities, infrastructure networks, housing development, and environmental protection strategies across Canada. Classified under NOC 21202 (TEER 1), this profession qualifies for skilled-immigration pathways such as Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), employer-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2120 – Architects, urban planners, and land surveyors

duties: Urban and Land Use Planners typically:
Analyze demographic, economic, legal, and environmental data
Consult with government officials, developers, and community groups
Design zoning, transportation, housing, and utility plans
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in urban planning, geography, architecture, engineering, or related field
Membership in the Canadian Institute of Planners
Provincial regulation applies in Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Alberta, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is projected to create 2,700 new Urban-Planner positions, with around 2,500 job seekers—signalling a stable and favorable immigration outlook, particularly for internationally trained professionals who secure provincial licensing and employer sponsorship.
additional_information: Urban-planning professionals remain in strong demand due to:
Housing-supply expansionTransit-oriented developmentClimate-resilience planningEnvironmental-protection initiativesMunicipal infrastructure growth
Federal, provincial, and municipal governmentsLand-development companiesEngineering & planning consultanciesEnvironmental agenciesPrivate urban-planning firms
Annual salaries commonly range between $55,500–$113,600 CAD, depending on province, licensing, and experience.
Why Choose Canada as an Urban Planner?

Stable public-sector hiringProvincial nomination opportunitiesLong-term infrastructure investmentPR-linked skilled-worker pathwaysUniversal healthcareFamily sponsorship optionsMulticultural professional communities
